task_completion.description: Verify that at least 3 new wall segments have been added to partition the open building shell.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sweet Home 3D 2D plan view, look for at least 3 distinct wall lines drawn inside the main building shell to separate the interior into different utilitarian areas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the four required zones are identified with text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 4 text labels in the 2D plan view specifically reading 'Rentals', 'Lockers', 'Lounge', and 'Cafeteria'.

task_completion.description: Verify the furnishing of the Rentals and Lockers zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D or 3D view, the Rentals zone must contain at least 6 storage units or service counters. The Lockers zone must contain at least 2 benches or stools.

task_completion.description: Verify the Warming Lounge features a fireplace/stove, seating, and a distinct floor texture/color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Lounge area must show at least 1 fireplace or stove and at least 4 lounge seats. Additionally, the floor in this specific room must have a distinct color or texture compared to the rest of the building, indicating a defined Room boundary was created.

task_completion.description: Verify the Cafeteria zone has dining furniture and a food service area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Cafeteria area must contain at least 3 dining tables and 10 chairs. There must also be a food service section with at least 2 appliances (e.g., refrigerator, oven, microwave, coffee maker).

task_completion.description: Verify the overall design contains at least 40 furniture items and is saved to the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The overall 2D/3D view should appear densely furnished, visibly containing 40 or more individual items from the catalog. The Sweet Home 3D window title or a save dialog/terminal should indicate the file is saved as 'ski_lodge.sh3d' in the '~/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open, with evidence of catalog browsing, drag-and-drop actions, and active use of the 2D plan and 3D view.

integrity.description: Verify that furniture and walls are placed logically within the environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Items should be distributed sensibly across the floor plan, not stacked on top of each other at a single coordinate (e.g., 0,0) or placed outside the building shell.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI by writing a script to generate the .sh3d XML file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show visual progression of the design within the Sweet Home 3D software, rather than a text editor or terminal being used to inject XML code (pieceOfFurniture, wall, room, label, floorTexture) into the save file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying the starter file 'ski_lodge_starter.sh3d'. The final saved file must contain specific XML elements corresponding to visual features: 'wall' (at least 3), 'label' (Rentals, Lockers, Lounge, Cafeteria), 'room' and 'floorTexture' (for the Lounge), and 'pieceOfFurniture' (at least 40 total, including specific counts for storage, benches, lounge seats, tables, chairs, and appliances). The pass threshold is 70/100 points.
